task_completion.description: The target color image is opened and visible in the GIMP workspace.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The original color image is displayed in the main GIMP image window.

task_completion.description: The Threshold tool dialog is opened.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The 'Threshold' dialog box (typically accessed via Colors -> Threshold) is visible on the screen.

task_completion.description: The image is converted to pure black and white.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The image on the canvas appears as a pure binary black and white image, with no colors and no grayscale mid-tones.

integrity.description: The agent used the GIMP GUI to perform the conversion.
integrity.visual_evidence: Screenshots show interaction with GIMP menus and dialogs rather than terminal commands or scripts.

integrity.description: The agent modified the original image rather than replacing it.
integrity.visual_evidence: The black and white image retains the recognizable content and composition of the original color image.

privileged_info_for_vlm: The task requires converting a color image to pure black and white using GIMP's Threshold tool. A successful conversion results in a binary image where ≥95% of pixels are either pure black (intensity ≤25) or pure white (intensity ≥230), with <5% mid-tones (80-175). The final image must be balanced, meaning neither black nor white dominates more than 90% of the image.
